titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University Track and Field collection40-9-1820
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-21 18:26:17 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Track and Field collection
did.abstract: Materials documenting the history of the track teams at Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials documenting the history of the track teams at Cornell. Collection contains a collection of training logs and results books as well as various programs and ephemera relating to events and operations of the track team. Many photos and slides of individual team members and events, some photos include trips to England. A collection of medals won by Walker Smith is included as well as another set won by Daniel B. Strickler. Material from Los Angeles, Antwerp, Berlin and Oslo Summer Olympic Games material from Cornell Olympians including film footage and photos. Winning jersey bib number plates worn by winners in track and field at the 1920 Antwerp Belgium Olympics, this includes non-U.S. and non-Cornell winners as well, some are signed. Many films of various track events and training sessions. Films include, but are not limited to: IC4A and Heptagonal track and field events, 1932; Penn Relays, 1955; 1952 Olympics. Also a film primarily concerning Cornell athletics, including track and field and baseball that includes Spring Day games, a parade, male students clowning, and the Green Dragon. Also pictorial log of the trip to England of the track teams of Cornell University and Princeton University. The album has photographs, clippings, and memorabilia documenting the Anglo-American Athletic Match track and field events held in England between Cornell and Princeton and Oxford and Cambridge in July 1930. Also programs from the Niagara Track & Field Hall of Fame, featuring Cornellians.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Walter Ashbaugh '51, Meredith Gourdine '52 and Charles H. Moore '51 competed at the 52 Olympics and may be featured on the film.

userestrict.head: Terms Governing Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Audio visual media that has been digitized: F-522 and F-524.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Track and Field collection, #40-9-1820. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Hunt Bradley papers, #41-2-1550, http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMA01550

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. General papers, photographs and ephemera Box 1, 7, 9, 10, Mapcase
Series II. Training and Results Books Box 2-3
Series III. Oversize Photographs Box 4-6, Mapcase
Series IV. Videos and Film Box 7-8

did.unittitle: Series I. General papers, photographs, and ephemera
NY state intercollegiate Track and Field meet. Rome, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One photo features many of the track coach contemporaries of Jack Moakley at an event. Also includes program from the March 24, 1923 Michigan Dual Meet.

did.unittitle: Disassembled collage featuring the number plates worn by track and field athletes on their jerseys at the 1920 Olympics in Antwerp Belgium who won gold medals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several of the number plates are autographed by the athletes. The plates include Frank Foss, (Cornell '17) signed, Joseph Guillemot, Albert Hill signed (also appears signed by Horace Hallock Brown, Hannes Kolehmainen, Richmond Landon signed, Frank Loomis signed (also signed by Frank Sheehan Cornell trainer, Pat McDonald signed, Charley Paddock, Bevil Rudd, Patrick Ryan signed, and Earl Thomson. The one for Allen Woodring is missing. There are also two that have name labels that do not correspond with who won gold. They are shot put labeled "Finnthuma" actually won by Ville Pörhölä and broad jump labeled "Finna" actually won by William Petersson. Also includes a picture of Jack Moakley and Frank Sheehan and a U.S. Olympic track team patch. See photo on CD-1941 (located in Box 1, as well) for picture before it was disassembled.

did.unittitle: Pieces of the 1920 Olympic collage from folder 7

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negatives of what appear to be the IC4A and English meets of 1949. Also slides of the 1949 English meet and training slides of shot put showing technique at all stages of the throw and other 1949 slides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some of the prints come from a film can of negatives in folder 1-44. They include photos of Meredith Gourdine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film cannister of negatives. Some of the prints from this can are in an envelope in folder 1-16. They include photos of Meredith Gourdine.

did.unittitle: Projector photograph slides of the 1950 England trip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Cornell and Princeton track teams for matches against Cambridge and Oxford. Includes photos of the matches, but also photos of the ships they traveled on, various landmarks, stops in Paris, climbing the Eiffel Tower, Luxembourg Gardens, Statue of Liberty. Roger Bannister would compete at one of the events.

did.unittitle: Charles Moore Jr., winner of a gold and silver medal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some slides.

did.unittitle: The Inside Track: Creating Success at Cornell, by Colin McClive
University of Missouri XC and Track and Field Camp
University of Missouri XC and Track and Field Camp
University of Missouri XC and Track and Field Camp
University of Missouri Speed Development Camp
University of Missouri XC and Track and Field Camp
H. Hunt Bradley Track Center Dedication
Track and Field News's Big Red Book
CD-1941, contains low resolution photographs of many of the photos in the collection when they were on the wall in Barton Hall track room, before they were unframed.
Niagara Track & Field Hall of Fame programs and tickets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of Cornellians in the Hall of Fame

did.unittitle: Niagara Track & Field Hall of Fame programs
University College Dublin Athletic Club Visit to Cornell University plaque
University of Birmingham and Kingston Upon Hull plaques

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A pictorial log of the trip to England of the track teams of Cornell University and Princeton University." The album has photographs, clippings, and memorabilia documenting the Anglo-American Athletic Match track and field events held in England between Cornell and Princeton and Oxford and Cambridge in July 1930.

did.unittitle: Glass plate negative of 2 Cornell runners - Henry Norton Stone and Frederic Kimball Lovejoy
Photos from the 1934 and 1938 England trip
Varsity track wearer of the C certificate of Clarence Francis Spindler
Photos and ephemera from the 1930 England trip

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Princeton's Alfred Mills to Cornell's Carl Henry Meinig mentioning the trip and a newsclipping from The Illustrated and Sporting Dramatic News, July 19, 1930.

did.unittitle: Track medals won by Walker Smith '20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 32 medals removed from mounting on a board. The medals are from a number of different competitions including, the Amateur Athletic Union of the United States, Svensk Amerikanska, IC4A, Meadowbrook Club of Philadelphia, Achilles Club of Britain, Queens Club of London, Boston Athletic Association, New York Athletic Club, Harvard, Yale, 1920 Olympics in Antwerp Belgium medal presented by John Hylan, mayor of New York and others.

did.unittitle: Track medals won by Daniel Strickler '22

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 medals, one for the 1 mile run at the Indoor Triangular Meet 1922 with Cornell, Dartmouth, Harvard meet, one from the International Track & Field Meet on Travers Island with Oxford, Cambridge, Princeton, Cornell meet and one a medallion with an image of runners on it..

did.unittitle: Wood frame engraved with former runners initials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A photo of Jack Moakley in front of a very similar, but different wood frame can be seen in Box 1 folder 41.

did.unittitle: Series II. Training and Results Books
IC4A 33rd Annual Field Meeting, Franklin Field, Philadelphia PA
IC4A 34th Annual Field Meeting, Soldiers Field, Cambridge MA
IC4A 35th Annual Field Meeting, Franklin Field, Philadelphia PA

IC4A 41st annual field meeting, Soldiers Field, Cambridge MA
IC4A 46th Annual Field Meeting, Soldiers Field, Cambridge MA
IC4A 50th Annual Field Meeting, Soldiers Field, Cambridge MA
IC4A Souvenir Volume of Annual Championship Meetings
IC4A Souvenir Volume of Annual Championship Meetings
IC4A Souvenir Volume of Annual Championship Meetings
IC4A Souvenir Volume of Annual Championship Meetings
Partial Photo of the Track Team
Cornell Hall of Fame Induction, George Miske, class of 1922
Cornell Hall of Fame Induction, Alma M Richards, class of 1917
Cornell Hall of Fame Induction, Robert E Brown, class of 1922
Track Team photo, plus four individual photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes things like the 1944-45 11th Annual Heptagonal Championship poster

did.unittitle: Large Cornell, Yale, Harvard, Navy Princeton, West Point, Dartmouth group photo
Track and Field motion picture film (1 reel, 16mm)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most footage for track and field events Also Includes: male students clowning, Spring Day games such as "Ring a duck" and "Nigger in a tub" Parade with floats (one, "Dean of Ithaca" truck) and the Dragon Field and track training and competition Baseball

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Good enough for a showing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including 1932 Olympics in Los Angeles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: University of California Berkeley, Edwards Field

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This film is in poor condition and can no longer be played or digitized.

dao.xlink:title: Barton Hall Indoor Heptagonal games [Staff access]

daodesc.p: Barton Hall Indoor Heptagonal games [Staff access]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This film is in poor condition and may no longer be playable.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This film is in poor condition and can no longer be played or digitized.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Film features footage of St. Paul's Cathedral and White Stadium where the track meet took place, as well as various buildings that had been partially destroyed during WWII.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May include film of Walter Ashbaugh '51, 4th in triple jump, Meredith Gourdine '53, Silver in long jump, Charles H Moore, Silver in relay and gold in hurdles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly footage of Roger Bannister breaking the four minute mile

did.unittitle: Cross Country Season under Coach Montgomery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes detailed description of each competitors technique on separate papers

did.unittitle: Belan Court and Informal Photos of Runners on Board Track
Cornell Track Rally, Fort Lee. Meet day, 2
Lou Montgomery, coach, includes practices of high jump and pole vault
IC4A Track and Field at Randall's Island NY
Fort Lee, half of the reel is good
Mikulina competing in London, High Jump 6'2-6'4
Cornell outdoor practice, includes shot-put, discus, hammer
Pole vault and hurdles, Elliot and Herman
Low hurdles, odd shots. G.H. Hart, Coach Montgomery
